constructors.name: PersistentSettings
constructors.summary: Initializes a new instance of the PersistentSettings class

methods.name: AddChild
methods.signature: public PersistentSettings AddChild(string key)
methods.summary: Call this method to add a new child key, if the key is exists then the existing key is returned otherwise a new empty PersistentSettings child key is added and the new settings are returned.

params.name: key
params.doc: Key to add to the child dictionary.

returns.doc: If the key is exists then the existing key is returned otherwise a new empty PersistentSettings child key is added and the new settings are returned.

methods.signature: public PersistentSettings GetChild(string key)
methods.summary: Call this method to get a nested settings PersistentSettings instance, will throw a KeyNotFoundException exception if the key does not exist.

returns.doc: Returns persistent settings for the specified key or throws an exception if the key is invalid.

throws.when: Thrown if the key does not exist.

methods.name: GetSettingIsHiddenFromUserInterface(String)
methods.signature: public bool GetSettingIsHiddenFromUserInterface(string key)
methods.summary: Values read from all users settings files will be marked as read-only which will cause any future calls to Set... to fail.

params.name: key
params.doc: Key name for which to search.

returns.doc: Returns true if the setting is read-only otherwise false.

throws.when: A KeyNotFoundException exception is thrown if the key is not found in the settings dictionary.

methods.signature: public bool GetSettingIsHiddenFromUserInterface(string key, IEnumerable<string> legacyKeyList)

throws.when: A KeyNotFoundException exception is thrown if the key is not found in the settings dictionary.

methods.name: GetValidator<T>
methods.signature: public EventHandler<PersistentSettingsEventArgs<T>> GetValidator<T>(string key)
methods.summary: Provides a way to find a ready-to-use validator for the PersistentSetting class for the given the key, or obtaining null.

params.name: key
params.doc: The name of the setting key.

returns.doc: A valid validator, or null if no validator was found.

throws.when: If type parameter T is not the right specialization for PersistentSettingsEventArgs<T>.

methods.name: HideSettingFromUserInterface
methods.signature: public void HideSettingFromUserInterface(string key)

methods.name: RegisterSettingsValidator<T>
methods.signature: public void RegisterSettingsValidator<T>(string key, EventHandler<PersistentSettingsEventArgs<T>> validator)
methods.summary: Sets a validator for a given key. Note to implementers: T should be one of the supported types for the PersistentSettings class and should match the type associated with the key.This method allows to use anonymous methods and lambda expressions.

params.name: key
params.doc: The key to which to bind the validator.

params.name: validator
params.doc: A validator instance of your own class.

methods.name: TryGetChild
methods.signature: public bool TryGetChild(string key, out PersistentSettings value)

params.doc: [out] Will be set the child settings if the key is valid otherwise it will be null.

returns.doc: Returns true if the key exists and value was set otherwise; returns false.

properties.name: ChildKeys
properties.summary: Gets a collection containing the keys in the settings dictionary.

properties.name: HiddenFromUserInterface
properties.summary: If false then values will appear in the EditOptions window

properties.name: Keys
properties.summary: Gets a collection containing the keys in the settings dictionary.

properties.name: StringListRootKey
properties.summary: Adding this string to a string list when calling SetStringList will cause the ProgramData setting to get inserted at that location in the list.
